title: Rotation warm-up
detail: 3-position turn drill to groove the swing motion before you start hitting.

steps.heading: Body motion (same for all three positions)
cues: Stand shoulder-width apart; left foot turned out slightly toward the target.
Head and eyes stay forward — no turning.
Turn your shoulders back to the right; lower slightly through the knees.
Turn through the hips forward and rotate to the front.
Right foot turns and finishes up on the toes.
Weight starts on the back foot and ends on the front foot.
Go slowly — a few reps at each position.

steps.heading: Position 1 — club behind the shoulders
cues: Hold the club behind your shoulders, one hand beside each shoulder, thumbs toward your body.
Run through the body motion above.

steps.heading: Position 2 — club in front, above the belt line
cues: Move the club to the front, just above the belt line.
Keep it level — it should only turn around your body, not move up and down.

steps.heading: Position 3 — club behind the body, above the belt line
cues: Move the club behind your body, above the belt line.
Same rotation, same level turn.

title: Grip check
detail: Build the same grip every time — it steers the clubface.

steps.heading: Left hand (top hand)
cues: Lay the grip across the base of your fingers, running to the base of your small finger.
Close your hand over the top like a handshake.
Looking down you should see about two knuckles of this hand.

steps.heading: Right hand (bottom hand)
cues: Fit the lifeline of your right palm over your left thumb.
Interlock the little finger of the right hand with the index finger of the left.
The 'V' made by your right thumb and index finger points up toward your right shoulder.

steps.heading: Pressure
cues: Hold light — like a tube of toothpaste you don't want to squeeze.
No white knuckles; tension kills the swing.

title: Half → full swings
detail: Build from waist-high swings to full, keeping the turn in control.

steps.heading: Set-up
cues: Feet about shoulder-width, slight knee flex, arms hanging.
Weight balanced, ball in the middle of your stance.

steps.heading: Half swing (9 to 3)
cues: Lead arm swings back to waist height (9 o'clock), club shaft parallel to the ground.
Turn through so your belt buckle faces the target and the club reaches 3 o'clock.
Both arms stay straight through the strike.

steps.heading: Three-quarter
cues: Lengthen the backswing to shoulder height; the trail elbow folds.
Same body turn — the arms don't lift on their own.

steps.heading: Full swing
cues: Turn the lead shoulder under your chin.
Stop the club before it dips down behind your back.
Finish balanced on your front foot, chest to the target.
Key cue: turn, don't lift — the club never goes past parallel behind you.

title: Alignment station
detail: Two clubs on the ground so your aim stays honest — fixes the #1 beginner miss.

steps.heading: Build the station
cues: Lay one club on the ground pointing straight at your target — this is the ball-to-target line.
Lay a second club parallel to it, just outside your toes (like two rail tracks).
Your feet, hips and shoulders line up along the toe club — aimed parallel-LEFT of the target, not at it. Only the clubface aims at the target.

steps.heading: Hit 5–10 balls
cues: Step in and match your feet to the toe club every time.
Check the clubface points at the target before each swing.
Reset your feet for every ball — don't get lazy.

steps.heading: Why
cues: Most beginners aim way right and never know it.
Honest aim makes every other fault easier to see.

title: Wedge warm-up ladder
detail: Find the middle of the face with small swings before you go full.

steps.heading: Start small
cues: Take a wedge or 9-iron.
Make smooth half swings (9 to 3) and hit 5 balls.
You're chasing clean contact, not distance.

steps.heading: Lengthen
cues: Only once 3 in a row feel solid, go to three-quarter, then full.
If contact gets sloppy, shorten the swing again.

steps.heading: Work up the bag
cues: Move up one club at a time: wedge → 7-iron → hybrid → driver.
Never start a session with the driver.
Smooth tempo beats swinging hard.

title: Pick-a-target routine
detail: Never hit an aimless ball — rehearse the routine you'll use on the course.

steps.heading: Every ball gets a target
cues: Pick a specific target for every single shot — a flag, a distance marker, a far tree.
No lazy raking of balls without aiming.

steps.heading: Pre-shot routine
cues: Stand behind the ball and look down the line to your target.
Pick a spot on the ground about a foot in front of the ball on that line; aim the clubface over it.
One practice swing, step in, one look at the target, then swing.

steps.heading: Reset
cues: Step away and repeat the whole routine for the next ball.
Same routine every time builds the habit you need on the course.
